market_description: AI-powered contract intelligence platform for mid-market legal and procurement teams, automating contract review, risk flagging, and clause extraction.
feature_list: Automated contract review with clause-level extraction
AI risk scoring per contract with plain-language summaries
Multi-party redlining workflow with tracked changes
Real-time obligation calendar and renewal alerts
Playbook enforcement engine to flag deviations from standard terms
Bulk contract ingestion from email and SharePoint

unique_feature_list.description: Automatically maps cross-contract dependencies — e.g., if Contract A's SLA depends on Vendor B's delivery schedule in Contract C — surfacing cascading risk no other tool tracks.
Scores counterparty assertiveness and predicts likely acceptance probability for each proposed redline, trained on 4M closed deal outcomes.
Shows the provenance of every clause — which template it originated from, who accepted it, and how many times it has been used across the portfolio.

target_customer: Mid-market legal ops and procurement teams at companies with 200–2000 employees
who_pays_vs_who_uses: Legal ops or procurement budget owner pays; in-house lawyers and contract managers use it daily
paying_segment: Legal Operations Manager or VP of Procurement
analyst_notes: Pre-revenue; 3 LOIs signed at $2,400 ARR/seat target. Pricing benchmarked against Ironclad (est. $150–$400/seat) and ContractPodAi (est. $200–$500/seat). Discounts planned for deal sizes >50 seats.
tech_description: Core NLP pipeline built on fine-tuned LegalBERT with a proprietary graph database layer (Neo4j-based) for cross-contract dependency mapping. Inference latency <200ms p95 on a 50-page contract. Obligation graph algorithm is novel — no public equivalent found in LegalTech literature.
